title: DNS Resolution Troubleshooting and Cache Flush
content:
DNS Resolution Troubleshooting and Cache Flush:

### Symptoms
- Log entries showing: 'socket.gaierror: [Errno -3] Temporary failure in name resolution'.
- Services unable to authenticate with OAuth/Identity providers due to unreachable URL.
- Curl requests failing with 'Could not resolve host'.

### Diagnosis
1. Verify if DNS resolution works from inside the container:
   kubectl exec <pod_name> -- nslookup auth-service.identity.provider
2. Inspect the resolver configurations file:
   kubectl exec <pod_name> -- cat /etc/resolv.conf

### Safe Mitigation Steps
Option A: Flush local CoreDNS cache
- If running inside Kubernetes, rollout CoreDNS deployment:
  kubectl rollout restart deployment coredns -n kube-system

Option B: Temporary resolver host bypass
- Inject direct host-IP mapping in Kubernetes deployment Spec or hosts file to bypass DNS temporarily:
  echo '192.168.10.45 auth-service.identity.provider' >> /etc/hosts

### Rollback Plan
Verify system connectivity using nslookup. Remove temporary /etc/hosts mappings once DNS resolvers recover.
